@code {
    [Parameter] public string  Env            { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    [Parameter] public string? NavigationHint { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public bool    IsFocused      { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public TabModel? Tab          { get; set; }

    // ── filter grammar for log lines ──────────────────────────────────────
    private static readonly FilterParser LogLineParser = new(
        searchableFields: ["Level", "Machine", "Message"],
        aliases: new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
        {
            ["ts"]        = "Timestamp",
            ["timestamp"] = "Timestamp",
            ["level"]     = "Level",
            ["machine"]   = "Machine",
            ["pid"]       = "Pid",
            ["thread"]    = "ThreadId",
            ["tid"]       = "ThreadId",
            ["msg"]       = "Message",
            ["message"]   = "Message",
            ["caller"]    = "Caller",
        });

    private static readonly (string Aliases, string Label)[] ContentFieldHints =
    [
        ("ts / timestamp", "entry timestamp"),
        ("level",          "INFO · WARN · ERROR · DEBUG"),
        ("machine",        "server / machine name"),
        ("pid",            "process id (integer)"),
        ("thread / tid",   "thread id (integer)"),
        ("msg / message",  "log message text"),
        ("caller",         "calling method"),
    ];

    // ── filter grammar for the always-visible row filter (filters the file table
    // itself — Server/FileName/Size/dates — distinct from the content filter above,
    // which filters log LINES inside files during a recursive search). ──────────
    private static readonly FilterParser FileRowParser = new(
        searchableFields: ["Server", "FileName"],
        aliases: new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
        {
            ["server"]   = "Server",
            ["host"]     = "Server",
            ["file"]     = "FileName",
            ["filename"] = "FileName",
            ["name"]     = "FileName",
            ["size"]     = "SizeBytes",
            ["created"]  = "CreatedUtc",
            ["updated"]  = "LastModifiedUtc",
            ["modified"] = "LastModifiedUtc",
            ["path"]     = "FullPath",
            ["fullpath"] = "FullPath",
        });

    private static readonly (string Aliases, string Label)[] RowFieldHints =
    [
        ("server / host",         "server / machine name"),
        ("file / filename / name", "file name"),
        ("size",                  "file size (bytes)"),
        ("created",               "creation time"),
        ("updated / modified",    "last modified time"),
        ("path / fullpath",       "full file path"),
    ];

    // ── panel resize ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    private ElementReference _bodyRef;
    private ElementReference _treePanelRef;
    private double            _treePanelWidth = 280;
    private DotNetObjectReference<FileBrowser>? _selfRef;

    [JSInvokable]
    public void SetTreeWidth(double width) =>
        _treePanelWidth = Math.Clamp(width, 160, 600);

    private async Task OnResizerMouseDown(MouseEventArgs e)
    {
        _selfRef ??= DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);
        await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("logBrowserResizer.start", e.ClientX, _selfRef, _bodyRef, _treePanelRef);
    }

    // ── tree state ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    private List<string>        _servers   = new();
    private List<LogFolderNode> _rootNodes = new();

    private readonly Dictionary<string, IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode>> _childCache =
        new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
    private readonly HashSet<string> _expanded = new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    private readonly LogFolderNode _loadingNode = new("…", "__loading__", false);

    // Sidebar tree filter — same shared filter grammar as every other filter box
    // (AND/OR/NOT/parens, see docs/08_FilterSyntax.md), evaluated against the folder's
    // full RelativePath by default (not just its own Name), so typing an ancestor
    // segment (e.g. "orders") surfaces every folder underneath it, not just folders
    // literally named "orders". A folder is kept if it matches OR any of its (loaded)
    // descendants match — see NodeMatchesOrHasDescendantMatch. Purely client-side:
    // operates only on whatever's already in _rootNodes/_childCache (i.e. branches the
    // user has manually expanded at some point) — typing a filter never itself triggers
    // a server call to go fetch unexplored branches just to search inside them.
    private static readonly FilterParser TreeFilterParser = new(
        searchableFields: ["RelativePath"],
        aliases: new Dictionary<string, string>(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
        {
            ["path"] = "RelativePath",
            ["name"] = "Name",
        });

    private static readonly (string Aliases, string Label)[] TreeFieldHints =
    [
        ("path", "full folder path (default)"),
        ("name", "folder name only"),
    ];

    private string      _treeFilterText  = "";
    private string?     _treeFilterError;
    private FilterNode? _treeFilterNode;

    private void OnTreeFilterChanged(string v)
    {
        _treeFilterText  = v;
        _treeFilterError = null;
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(v)) { _treeFilterNode = null; return; }
        try   { _treeFilterNode = TreeFilterParser.Parse(v, DtSvc.UseUtc); }
        catch (Exception ex) { _treeFilterError = ex.Message; _treeFilterNode = null; }
    }

    private List<LogFolderNode> FilteredRootNodes =>
        _treeFilterNode is null
            ? _rootNodes
            : _rootNodes.Where(NodeMatchesOrHasDescendantMatch).ToList();

    private Dictionary<string, IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode>> FilteredChildCache =>
        _treeFilterNode is null
            ? _childCache
            : _childCache.ToDictionary(
                kv => kv.Key,
                kv => (IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode>)kv.Value.Where(NodeMatchesOrHasDescendantMatch).ToList(),
                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    // A folder survives the filter if it matches itself, or any (already-loaded)
    // descendant matches — otherwise a non-matching parent would hide matching
    // children beneath it entirely.
    private bool NodeMatchesOrHasDescendantMatch(LogFolderNode n) =>
        FilterEvaluator.Evaluate(_treeFilterNode, n) ||
        (_childCache.TryGetValue(n.RelativePath, out var children) && children.Any(NodeMatchesOrHasDescendantMatch));

    // While filtering, auto-expand every node that has a surviving (kept) child, so the
    // match is actually visible without the user manually expanding each ancestor.
    private HashSet<string> EffectiveExpanded =>
        _treeFilterNode is null
            ? _expanded
            : new HashSet<string>(
                _expanded.Concat(FilteredChildCache.Where(kv => kv.Value.Count > 0).Select(kv => kv.Key)),
                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

    // ── file / search state ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Checked folders (checkbox column) — independent of _expanded (which folders are
    // open in the tree) and independent of each other; the files table aggregates
    // results from every checked path across all servers.
    private readonly HashSet<string> _checkedPaths = new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
    private List<LogFileEntry> _files        = new();
    private bool               _loadingFiles;
    private bool               _initialized;
    private DateTime?          _lastScan;
    private string?            _lastHint;
    private string?            _initError;
    // tracks which tree nodes are currently fetching their children
    private readonly HashSet<string> _loadingNodes = new(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
    // Bumped every time the checked-path set changes — lets an in-flight LoadFilesAsync
    // detect it's been superseded by a newer selection change and discard its result.
    private int _loadGeneration;

    // always-visible row filter — filters the currently-loaded file table itself
    private string      _rowFilterText  = "";
    private string?     _rowFilterError;
    private FilterNode? _rowFilterNode;

    private List<LogFileEntry> FilteredFiles =>
        _rowFilterNode is null ? _files : _files.Where(f => FilterEvaluator.Evaluate(_rowFilterNode, f)).ToList();

    private void OnRowFilterChanged(string v)
    {
        _rowFilterText  = v;
        _rowFilterError = null;
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(v)) { _rowFilterNode = null; return; }
        try   { _rowFilterNode = FileRowParser.Parse(v, DtSvc.UseUtc); }
        catch (Exception ex) { _rowFilterError = ex.Message; _rowFilterNode = null; }
    }

    // collapsible search bar (second toolbar row)
    private bool _searchBarOpen;

    private async Task ToggleSearchBarAsync()
    {
        _searchBarOpen = !_searchBarOpen;
        RestartPollingIfBrowsing();
        await Task.CompletedTask;
    }

    // search — placeholder until OnInitializedAsync sets it from LogSettings.DefaultFileGlob
    // (injected properties aren't available yet at field-initializer time).
    private string    _fileGlob           = "*";
    private string    _contentFilterStr   = "";
    private FilterNode? _contentFilterNode;
    private string?   _contentFilterError;
    private bool      _isSearching;
    private bool      _isSearchMode;      // true = table shows recursive search results

    private CancellationTokenSource  _cts        = new();
    private CancellationTokenSource? _searchCts;
    private FocusGatedPoller?        _poller;
    private bool                     _previousIsFocused;
    private bool                     _searchCapped;

    // Scroll-position preservation for the file table across auto-poll refreshes.
    private ElementReference _filesPanelRef;
    private bool             _restoreScroll;

    // ── lifecycle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        var interval = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(Math.Max(5, LogSettings.Value.FolderScanIntervalSeconds));
        _poller = new FocusGatedPoller(interval, async ct =>
        {
            // Skip auto-refresh while a search is running. Preserve scroll so the
            // periodic file-list refresh doesn't yank the user back to the top.
            if (_checkedPaths.Count > 0 && !_isSearching && !_isSearchMode)
                await InvokeAsync(() => LoadFilesAsync(preserveScroll: true));
        }, ex => Logger.LogWarning(ex, "Log browser auto-refresh failed"));

        using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
        _servers  = EnvironmentService.GetServers(Env).ToList();
        _fileGlob = DefaultFileGlob;

        try
        {
            var roots  = await BrowserSvc.GetSubfoldersAsync(_servers, "", _cts.Token);
            _rootNodes = roots.ToList();
        }
        catch (Exception ex) { _initError = ex.Message; }

        _initialized = true;
        _lastScan    = DateTime.UtcNow;
        _previousIsFocused = IsFocused;
        // No auto-start here — polling only begins once the user actually selects a
        // folder or toggles the search bar (see RestartPollingIfBrowsing).

        // Root is always expanded by default so the top-level folder list is visible
        // immediately, same as before "Root" existed as an explicit node.
        _expanded.Add("");
        await NavigateToStartupFolderAsync();
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Expands the tree down to the configured StartupFolder (relative to Root,
    /// {today} substituted — see ILogBrowserService.ResolveStartupPath), one segment
    /// at a time, stopping silently at the first segment that doesn't exist. Does not
    /// check/select the folder — matches the old hardcoded "auto-expand today's date
    /// folder" behaviour this replaces (expand only, not select).
    /// </summary>
    private async Task NavigateToStartupFolderAsync()
    {
        var startup = BrowserSvc.ResolveStartupPath();
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(startup)) return;

        var segments = startup.Split('\\', StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        var current = "";

        foreach (var seg in segments)
        {
            IReadOnlyList<LogFolderNode>? siblings = current == ""
                ? _rootNodes
                : _childCache.TryGetValue(current, out var cached) ? cached : null;

            if (siblings is null)
            {
                siblings = await BrowserSvc.GetSubfoldersAsync(_servers, current, _cts.Token);
                _childCache[current] = siblings;
            }

            var match = siblings.FirstOrDefault(n => n.Name.Equals(seg, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
            if (match is null) break; // segment doesn't exist — stop drilling, keep what resolved so far

            current = string.IsNullOrEmpty(current) ? seg : current + "\\" + seg;
            _expanded.Add(current);

            // Fetch this node's own children too, not just as a lookahead for the next
            // segment — without this, the deepest (last) segment gets marked expanded
            // but its children never get cached, so the tree shows it as expanded with
            // nothing underneath until the user manually collapses and re-expands it
            // (which is what triggers OnExpandedChangedAsync's own fetch below).
            if (!_childCache.ContainsKey(current))
                _childCache[current] = await BrowserSvc.GetSubfoldersAsync(_servers, current, _cts.Token);
        }
    }

    // Synchronous and separate from OnParametersSetAsync below — this must not
    // block (fire-and-forget only) so activating a tab never stalls on it.
    protected override void OnParametersSet()
    {
        if (IsFocused == _previousIsFocused) return;
        _previousIsFocused = IsFocused;

        if (IsFocused)
        {
            // Resume auto-refresh only if the user had already selected a folder before
            // navigating away and isn't composing a search — don't restart polling just
            // because the tab regained focus.
            RestartPollingIfBrowsing();
            if (_initialized && _checkedPaths.Count > 0 && !_isSearching && !_isSearchMode)
                _ = InvokeAsync(async () =>
                {
                    try { await LoadFilesAsync(); }
                    catch (Exception ex) { Logger.LogWarning(ex, "Log browser focus refresh failed"); }
                });
        }
        else
        {
            _poller?.Stop();
        }
    }

    // Auto-refresh only runs while the user is actively browsing a selected folder and
    // the search bar isn't open (composing/viewing a search is a snapshot, not live).
    private void RestartPollingIfBrowsing()
    {
        _poller?.SetActive(IsFocused && _checkedPaths.Count > 0 && !_searchBarOpen);
    }

    protected override async Task OnAfterRenderAsync(bool firstRender)
    {
        if (_restoreScroll)
        {
            _restoreScroll = false;
            try { await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("bmScrollKeep.restore", _filesPanelRef); }
            catch (JSDisconnectedException) { }
        }
    }

    protected override async Task OnParametersSetAsync()
    {
        if (NavigationHint is not null && NavigationHint != _lastHint)
        {
            _lastHint = NavigationHint;
            // Deliberately not gated on _initialized: OnParametersSetAsync can fire while
            // OnInitializedAsync's own GetSubfoldersAsync call is still in flight (Blazor
            // dispatches a fresh parameter update to an already-constructing component
            // instance the moment a second SetParametersAsync arrives, independent of
            // whether our own _initialized field has flipped true yet). Gating on it here
            // used to silently drop the hint whenever that race lost — and since _lastHint
            // was already updated, retrying the same link did nothing either; only closing
            // and reopening the tab (a fresh component instance) gave it another chance.
            // ApplyHintAsync is self-sufficient — it loads whatever tree data it needs
            // on demand — so it doesn't actually need to wait for init to finish first.
            await ApplyHintAsync(NavigationHint);
        }
    }

    // ── filter inputs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private string DefaultFileGlob =>
        string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(LogSettings.Value.DefaultFileGlob) ? "*" : LogSettings.Value.DefaultFileGlob;

    private void OnGlobChanged(string v) => _fileGlob = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(v) ? DefaultFileGlob : v;

    private void OnContentFilterChanged(string v)
    {
        _contentFilterStr   = v;
        _contentFilterError = null;
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(v)) { _contentFilterNode = null; return; }
        try   { _contentFilterNode = LogLineParser.Parse(v, DtSvc.UseUtc); }
        catch (Exception ex) { _contentFilterError = ex.Message; _contentFilterNode = null; }
    }

    // ── search bar: Start Search / Stop button ─────────────────────────────

    private async Task OnSearchButtonClickAsync()
    {
        if (_isSearching) StopSearch();
        else               await StartSearchAsync();
    }

    // ── tree interaction ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

    // Checking a folder is entirely independent from expanding it — clicking a row
    // only ever toggles expand/collapse (below); the checkbox is the sole way to
    // add/remove a folder from the file table's result set.
    private Task OnCheckChangedAsync(LogFolderNode node, bool isChecked) =>
        SetCheckedAsync(node.RelativePath, isChecked);

    private async Task SetCheckedAsync(string relativePath, bool isChecked)
    {
        // Checking/unchecking always exits search mode — it's a browsing action.
        if (_isSearching) StopSearch();
        _isSearchMode = false;

        if (isChecked) _checkedPaths.Add(relativePath);
        else           _checkedPaths.Remove(relativePath);

        await LoadFilesAsync();
        RestartPollingIfBrowsing();
    }

    private async Task OnItemClickAsync(LogFolderNode node)
    {
        if (node.HasChildren)
        {
            var isExpanded = _expanded.Contains(node.RelativePath);
            await OnExpandedChangedAsync(node, !isExpanded);
        }

        // Clicking a folder to open it also checks it, so its files show up in the
        // table without a separate checkbox click. Never unchecks on click — that
        // stays the checkbox's job, so collapsing a folder doesn't drop its results.
        if (!_checkedPaths.Contains(node.RelativePath))
            await SetCheckedAsync(node.RelativePath, true);
    }

    private async Task OnExpandedChangedAsync(LogFolderNode node, bool expanded)
    {
        if (expanded)
        {
            _expanded.Add(node.RelativePath);
            if (!_childCache.ContainsKey(node.RelativePath))
            {
                _loadingNodes.Add(node.RelativePath);
                _loadingFiles = true;
                using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
                StateHasChanged();
                await Task.Yield();
                try
                {
                    var children = await BrowserSvc.GetSubfoldersAsync(_servers, node.RelativePath, _cts.Token);
                    _childCache[node.RelativePath] = children;
                }
                finally
                {
                    _loadingNodes.Remove(node.RelativePath);
                    _loadingFiles = _loadingNodes.Count > 0;
                    StateHasChanged();
                }
            }
        }
        else
        {
            _expanded.Remove(node.RelativePath);
        }
    }

    // ── selection (checked folders) ───────────────────────────────────────

    private void ClearSelection()
    {
        if (_isSearching) StopSearch();
        _isSearchMode = false;
        _checkedPaths.Clear();
        _files.Clear();
        _poller?.Stop();
        StateHasChanged();
    }

    // ── hint navigation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private async Task ApplyHintAsync(string fullPath)
    {
        var parsed = BrowserSvc.ParseHintPath(fullPath, _servers);
        if (parsed is null) return;

        var segments = parsed.Value.RelativePath.Split('\\', StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        var current  = "";
        foreach (var seg in segments)
        {
            if (!_childCache.ContainsKey(current))
            {
                var ch = await BrowserSvc.GetSubfoldersAsync(_servers, current, _cts.Token);
                _childCache[current] = ch;
                if (current == "") _rootNodes = ch.ToList();
            }
            current = string.IsNullOrEmpty(current) ? seg : current + "\\" + seg;
            _expanded.Add(current);
        }

        _checkedPaths.Add(parsed.Value.RelativePath);
        await LoadFilesAsync();
        StateHasChanged();
    }

    // ── file loading (aggregated across all checked folders) ──────────────

    private async Task LoadFilesAsync(bool preserveScroll = false)
    {
        var myGen = ++_loadGeneration;

        if (_checkedPaths.Count == 0)
        {
            _files        = [];
            _loadingFiles = false;
            StateHasChanged();
            return;
        }

        _loadingFiles = true;
        StateHasChanged();
        await Task.Yield();

        var paths   = _checkedPaths.ToList(); // capture — user may change selection while awaiting
        var pending = new System.Collections.Concurrent.ConcurrentBag<LogFileEntry>();
        using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
        try
        {
            // Collect all (path, server) combinations in parallel without touching _files mid-flight.
            await Task.WhenAll(paths.SelectMany(path => _servers.Select(async server =>
            {
                var results = await BrowserSvc.GetFilesAsync([server], path, _cts.Token);
                foreach (var r in results) pending.Add(r);
            })));

            // Swap in the new list atomically — no intermediate empty-table flash.
            // preserveScroll (auto-poll only) captures scrollTop right before the swap and
            // restores it in OnAfterRenderAsync so a background refresh is imperceptible.
            await InvokeAsync(async () =>
            {
                if (myGen != _loadGeneration) return; // superseded by a newer selection change
                if (preserveScroll)
                {
                    try { await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("bmScrollKeep.save", _filesPanelRef); }
                    catch (JSDisconnectedException) { }
                    _restoreScroll = true;
                }
                _files    = [.. pending];
                _lastScan = DateTime.UtcNow;
                StateHasChanged();
            });
        }
        finally
        {
            if (myGen == _loadGeneration)
            {
                _loadingFiles = _loadingNodes.Count > 0;
                await InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged);
            }
        }
    }

    // ── search (recursive mode, across all checked folders) ────────────────

    private async Task StartSearchAsync()
    {
        if (_checkedPaths.Count == 0) return;
        if (_contentFilterError is not null) return;

        StopSearch();

        _searchCts    = CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource(_cts.Token);
        _isSearching  = true;
        _isSearchMode = true;
        _loadingFiles = true;
        _searchCapped = false;
        _files.Clear();
        using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
        StateHasChanged();

        var paths      = _checkedPaths.ToList();
        var glob       = _fileGlob;
        var filter     = _contentFilterNode;
        var ct         = _searchCts.Token;
        var maxResults = LogSettings.Value.MaxSearchResults;

        // Batches UI updates instead of one StateHasChanged per match — a recursive search
        // over a large tree can yield matches far faster than a render round trip keeps up
        // with, and doing one full render per match is what made the tab feel unresponsive
        // during a big search. All callbacks below run through InvokeAsync, which marshals
        // onto Blazor Server's single renderer sync context, so this shared state is safe
        // even though multiple checked-path searches run in parallel.
        var lastFlush = DateTime.UtcNow;
        var flushInterval = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(200);

        try
        {
            await Task.WhenAll(paths.Select(async path =>
            {
                await foreach (var entry in BrowserSvc.SearchAsync(_servers, path, glob, filter, ct))
                {
                    var reachedCap = false;
                    await InvokeAsync(() =>
                    {
                        if (_files.Count >= maxResults) return;
                        _files.Add(entry);
                        if (_files.Count >= maxResults)
                        {
                            _searchCapped = true;
                            reachedCap = true;
                        }
                        if (reachedCap || DateTime.UtcNow - lastFlush >= flushInterval)
                        {
                            lastFlush = DateTime.UtcNow;
                            StateHasChanged();
                        }
                    });
                    if (reachedCap) { await _searchCts.CancelAsync(); break; }
                }
            }));
            _lastScan = DateTime.UtcNow;
        }
        catch (OperationCanceledException) { }
        finally
        {
            _isSearching  = false;
            _loadingFiles = false;
            await InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged);
        }
    }

    private void StopSearch()
    {
        _searchCts?.Cancel();
        _searchCts?.Dispose();
        _searchCts   = null;
        _isSearching = false;
    }

    // ── refresh / polling ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
        if (_checkedPaths.Count > 0)
        {
            // Invalidate each checked folder's child cache entry and reload files.
            foreach (var path in _checkedPaths.ToList())
            {
                _childCache.Remove(path);
                var children = await BrowserSvc.GetSubfoldersAsync(_servers, path, _cts.Token);
                _childCache[path] = children;
            }
            await LoadFilesAsync();
        }
        else
        {
            // Nothing checked — refresh just the root listing.
            var roots  = await BrowserSvc.GetSubfoldersAsync(_servers, "", _cts.Token);
            _rootNodes = roots.ToList();
            _lastScan  = DateTime.UtcNow;
            StateHasChanged();
        }
    }

    // ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private async Task OpenInViewer(LogFileEntry file)
    {
        var mode = LogSettings.Value.FileAccessMode;

        if (mode != LogFileAccessMode.ServerOnly)
        {
            var key = await TryOpenClientSideAsync(file);
            if (key is not null)
            {
                var tab = TabModel.CreateLogWorkspace("local:" + key, Env);
                tab.Label      = file.FileName;
                tab.NoNavigate = true;
                TabService.OpenOrFocus(tab);
                return;
            }

            if (mode == LogFileAccessMode.ClientOnly)
            {
                Notifications.Report(
                    $"Client-side file access failed for \"{file.FileName}\" — see browser console for details",
                    source: "File Browser");
                return;
            }
        }

        // ServerOnly, or ClientWithFallback's fallback: server reads the file and ships
        // it over the circuit — either the browser doesn't support the File System
        // Access API, the user cancelled the one-time folder grant, or the granted
        // folder didn't actually contain this file (wrong folder granted).
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateLogWorkspace(file.FullPath, Env));
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Tries to read <paramref name="file"/> directly in the browser instead of having
    /// the server read it. Returns a window._localFiles key on success, null on any
    /// failure (caller falls back to the normal server-read path).
    /// </summary>
    private async Task<string?> TryOpenClientSideAsync(LogFileEntry file)
    {
        if (BrowserSvc.ParseHintPath(file.FullPath, _servers) is not (var server, var relPath))
            return null;

        var lastSep = relPath.LastIndexOfAny(['\\', '/']);
        var relDir  = lastSep >= 0 ? relPath[..lastSep] : string.Empty;
        var rootTemplate = LogSettings.Value.RootFolder;

        var key = await JS.InvokeAsync<string?>("bmLogFileAccess.tryOpenSilently", server, rootTemplate, relDir, file.FileName);
        key ??= await JS.InvokeAsync<string?>("bmLogFileAccess.openWithGrant", server, rootTemplate, relDir, file.FileName);
        return key;
    }

    private void OpenInWorkspace(LogFileEntry file) =>
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateLogWorkspace(file.FullPath, Env));

    private async Task OpenLocalFileAsync()
    {
        _selfRef ??= DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);
        await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("bmOpenLocalLogFile", _selfRef);
    }

    [JSInvokable]
    public void OnLocalFileOpened(string key, string filename)
    {
        InvokeAsync(() =>
        {
            var label = System.IO.Path.GetFileName(filename) is { Length: > 0 } n ? n : filename;
            var tab   = TabModel.CreateLogWorkspace("local:" + key, Env);
            tab.Label      = label;
            tab.NoNavigate = true;
            TabService.OpenOrFocus(tab);
        });
    }


    private async Task CopyPathAsync(string path) =>
        await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("copyText", path, "Path copied to clipboard");


    private static string FormatSize(long bytes)
    {
        if (bytes < 1024)      return FormattableString.Invariant($"{bytes} B");
        if (bytes < 1_048_576) return FormattableString.Invariant($"{bytes / 1024.0:F1} KB");
        return FormattableString.Invariant($"{bytes / 1_048_576.0:F1} MB");
    }

    public async ValueTask DisposeAsync()
    {
        StopSearch();
        _poller?.Stop();
        _selfRef?.Dispose();
        await _cts.CancelAsync();
        _cts.Dispose();
    }
}

@namespace NxtUI.Web.Pages
<div class="bm-help-page bm-help-page--tab">

    <div class="bm-help-header">
        <h1 class="bm-help-title">Filter syntax</h1>
        <p class="bm-help-lead">
            Every filter box in NxtUI speaks the same language.
            Type one or many terms, use operators to combine them, and prefix a
            term with a field name to narrow down where it looks.
        </p>
    </div>

    <!-- ── Combining terms ─────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Combining terms</h2>

        <p>
            By default, terms separated by a <strong>space</strong> are ANDed together —
            every term must match. Use a <strong>comma</strong> to OR terms — any one must match.
        </p>

        <table class="bm-help-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Syntax</th><th>Meaning</th><th>Example</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td><code>a b</code> or <code>a &amp; b</code></td><td>AND — both must match</td><td><code>Loader Transform</code></td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>a , b</code> or <code>a | b</code></td><td>OR — either must match</td><td><code>Loader , Parser</code></td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>!a</code></td><td>NOT — must not match</td><td><code>!error</code></td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>(…)</code></td><td>group to control precedence</td><td><code>Loader (error , timeout)</code></td></tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <div class="bm-help-callout">
            <strong>Precedence:</strong> NOT binds tightest, then AND (space), then OR (comma).
            So <code>car bike , plane dog</code> means <em>(car AND bike) OR (plane AND dog)</em>.
            Use parentheses when in doubt.
        </div>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Text matching ───────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Text matching</h2>

        <table class="bm-help-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Syntax</th><th>Meaning</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr>
                    <td><code>field:value</code></td>
                    <td>Case-insensitive <strong>contains</strong> — "Loader" matches "BatchLoader", "loaderMain", etc.</td>
                </tr>
                <tr>
                    <td><code>field:"Value"</code></td>
                    <td>Double quotes → case-<strong>sensitive</strong> contains</td>
                </tr>
                <tr>
                    <td><code>field:'value'</code></td>
                    <td>Single quotes → case-<strong>insensitive</strong> contains (same as no quotes)</td>
                </tr>
                <tr>
                    <td><code>field:Load*</code></td>
                    <td><strong>Glob</strong> — <code>*</code> matches any number of characters, <code>?</code> matches exactly one</td>
                </tr>
                <tr>
                    <td><code>field:=Value</code></td>
                    <td><strong>Exact</strong> match (whole value, case-insensitive). Wildcards not allowed here.</td>
                </tr>
                <tr>
                    <td><code>field:null</code></td>
                    <td>Field is <strong>null or missing</strong></td>
                </tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <div class="bm-help-callout">
            <strong>Tip:</strong> a term with no <code>field:</code> prefix searches across
            <em>all</em> text fields in the current view. <code>Loader</code> alone will
            match any record where any searchable field contains "Loader".
        </div>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Field-scoped groups ─────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Field-scoped groups</h2>
        <p>
            You can give several values for the same field using parentheses after the colon.
            Space inside the group means AND; comma means OR.
        </p>
        <table class="bm-help-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Example</th><th>Meaning</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td><code>svc:(Loader Transform)</code></td><td>service contains "Loader" AND "Transform"</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>svc:(Loader , Transform)</code></td><td>service is "Loader" OR "Transform"</td></tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Comparisons ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Comparisons &amp; ranges</h2>

        <p>Use comparison operators before the value for numeric and date fields.</p>

        <table class="bm-help-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Syntax</th><th>Meaning</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td><code>field:&gt;100</code></td><td>Greater than 100</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>field:&gt;=100</code></td><td>Greater than or equal to 100</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>field:&lt;100</code></td><td>Less than 100</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>field:&lt;=100</code></td><td>Less than or equal to 100</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>field:10..50</code></td><td>Between 10 and 50 <strong>inclusive</strong></td></tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Dates ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Dates &amp; times</h2>

        <p>All date/time values are interpreted as UTC.</p>

        <table class="bm-help-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Form</th><th>Example</th><th>Meaning</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td>Named</td><td><code>today</code>, <code>yesterday</code>, <code>now</code></td><td>Start of today / yesterday / current instant (UTC)</td></tr>
                <tr><td>Negative offset</td><td><code>-30s</code>, <code>-30m</code>, <code>-2h</code>, <code>-7d</code>, <code>-1w</code></td><td><strong>now − N</strong> — a real point in the past (e.g. for timestamps). Units: <code>s</code> seconds, <code>m</code> minutes, <code>h</code> hours, <code>d</code> days, <code>w</code> weeks</td></tr>
                <tr><td>Positive offset</td><td><code>30s</code>, <code>30m</code>, <code>2h</code>, <code>7d</code>, <code>1w</code></td><td><strong>today + N</strong> — midnight plus the interval (e.g. for age/duration fields)</td></tr>
                <tr><td>Absolute date</td><td><code>2024-06-15</code></td><td>Midnight UTC on that date</td></tr>
                <tr><td>Absolute datetime</td><td><code>2024-06-15T09:30</code></td><td>Specific UTC date and time</td></tr>
                <tr><td>Time of day</td><td><code>09:30</code>, <code>00:10:00</code></td><td><strong>hh:mm:ss</strong> — compares only the time portion of a timestamp, ignoring the date</td></tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <p>Combine with comparison operators or <code>..</code> ranges:</p>
        <div class="bm-help-examples">
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>start:&gt;-7d</code> <span>— started in the last 7 days (now − 7d)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>start:2024-01-01..2024-12-31</code> <span>— started in 2024</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>start:&gt;=09:00 start:&lt;17:00</code> <span>— started during business hours today</span></div>
        </div>

        <div class="bm-help-callout" style="margin-top:14px;">
            <strong>Negative vs positive offsets:</strong> <code>-10m</code> means <em>now minus 10 minutes</em> — a real point in
            the past, useful for timestamp fields like <code>start</code> or <code>updated</code>.  <code>10m</code> (no minus) means
            <em>today at 00:10:00</em>.  <code>hh:mm</code> compares only the time-of-day portion of the field, ignoring the date —
            useful for log timestamps (<code>ts</code>) or filtering events by time of day regardless of when they occurred.
        </div>

        <p style="margin-top:14px;">Timestamp fields (<code>start</code>, <code>finish</code>, <code>updated</code>) support negative offsets and date ranges:</p>
        <div class="bm-help-examples">
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>updated:&gt;-10m</code>   <span>— heartbeat received in the last 10 min (online)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>updated:&lt;-4m</code>    <span>— last heartbeat more than 4 min ago (stale)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>start:&gt;=09:00 start:&lt;17:00</code> <span>— events that started between 09:00 and 17:00 (any day)</span></div>
        </div>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Timeline fields ─────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Timeline fields</h2>
        <p>Use either the short alias or the full name — they are interchangeable.</p>

        <table class="bm-help-table bm-help-table--fields">
            <thead><tr><th>Alias</th><th>Full name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td><code>svc</code></td><td><code>service</code></td><td>Service name</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>pipe</code></td><td><code>pipeline</code></td><td>Pipeline name</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>src</code></td><td><code>source</code></td><td>Source identifier</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>chunk</code></td><td><code>name</code></td><td>Chunk name</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>srv</code></td><td><code>server</code></td><td>Server / host name</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>pid</code></td><td><code>processId</code></td><td>Process ID</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>start</code></td><td><code>startUtc</code></td><td>Event start time (UTC)</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>finish</code></td><td><code>finishUtc</code></td><td>Event finish time (UTC)</td></tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Run list fields ───────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Run list fields</h2>

        <table class="bm-help-table bm-help-table--fields">
            <thead><tr><th>Alias</th><th>Full name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td><code>run / runid</code></td><td><code>RunId</code></td><td>Run identifier</td></tr>

            </tbody>
        </table>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Services fields ───────────────────────────────────────────── -->

        <h2>Services fields</h2>

        <table class="bm-help-table bm-help-table--fields">
            <thead><tr><th>Alias</th><th>Full name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td><code>svcname / servicename</code></td><td><code>ServiceName</code></td><td>Service name</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>host / hostname</code></td><td><code>HostName</code></td><td>Host name</td></tr>

            </tbody>
        </table>

        <div class="bm-help-examples">
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>updated:&gt;-30s</code>  <span>— heartbeat in the last 30 seconds</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>updated:&gt;-10m</code>  <span>— heartbeat in the last 10 minutes (online)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>updated:&lt;-4m</code>   <span>— last heartbeat more than 4 minutes ago (stale)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>ram:&gt;500</code>        <span>— services using more than 500 MB</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>host:server1</code>    <span>— services running on server1</span></div>
        </div>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Log viewer fields ────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Log viewer fields</h2>
        <p>Use either the alias or the full field name — they are interchangeable.</p>

        <table class="bm-help-table bm-help-table--fields">
            <thead><tr><th>Alias</th><th>Full name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
                <tr><td><code>ts</code></td><td><code>timestamp</code></td><td>Log timestamp — supports absolute dates, negative offsets, and <code>hh:mm:ss</code> time-of-day</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>lvl</code></td><td><code>level</code></td><td>Log level (INFO, WARN, ERROR, DEBUG)</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>msg</code></td><td><code>message</code></td><td>Log message text</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>tid</code></td><td><code>threadId</code></td><td>Thread ID — numeric comparisons supported</td></tr>
                <tr><td><code>pid</code></td><td><code>pid</code></td><td>Process ID — numeric comparisons supported</td></tr>
                <tr><td>—</td><td><code>host</code></td><td>Host / server name</td></tr>
                <tr><td>—</td><td><code>caller</code></td><td>Caller (class.method)</td></tr>
            </tbody>
        </table>

        <div class="bm-help-examples">
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>lvl:ERROR</code>              <span>— only error entries</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>ts:&gt;19:00</code>             <span>— entries after 19:00 (time-of-day, any date)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>ts:09:00..17:00</code>        <span>— entries between 09:00 and 17:00 (any date)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>ts:&gt;2024-06-01</code>        <span>— entries after 1 June 2024</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>lvl:ERROR msg:timeout</code>  <span>— error entries mentioning "timeout"</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>tid:&gt;10 tid:&lt;50</code>     <span>— entries from threads 11–49</span></div>
        </div>
    </section>

    <!-- ── Examples ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
    <section class="bm-help-section">
        <h2>Examples</h2>

        <div class="bm-help-examples">
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>Loader</code>
                <span>— any event whose service, pipeline, source, chunk, or server contains "Loader"</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>svc:Loader</code>
                <span>— service contains "Loader" (case-insensitive)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>svc:"Loader"</code>
                <span>— service contains "Loader" (case-sensitive)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>svc:=Loader</code>
                <span>— service is exactly "Loader"</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>svc:Load*</code>
                <span>— service starts with "Load" (glob)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>svc:Loader !error</code>
                <span>— service contains "Loader" AND no field contains "error"</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>svc:(Loader , Parser)</code>
                <span>— service is "Loader" or "Parser"</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>pipe:null</code>
                <span>— events with no pipeline assigned</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>start:&gt;-1h</code>
                <span>— events that started in the last hour</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>start:&gt;=09:00 start:&lt;17:00</code>
                <span>— events that started during business hours today (UTC)</span></div>
            <div class="bm-help-ex"><code>svc:Loader start:&gt;-1h</code>
                <span>— Loader events that started in the last hour</span></div>
        </div>
    </section>

</div>

                </div>

        </div>

    </div>

    @if (HomeOpts.Value.ShowMemoryDashboard)

                     OnOpenDetail="@(r => OpenDetail(r))"
                     OnOpenTimeline="@(r => OpenTimeline(r))"

@code {
    // Batch state
    private List<RunSummary> _recent = new();

    // Health state
    private KafkaHealth? _kafkaHealth = null;
    private MongoHealth? _mongoHealth = null;

    private CancellationTokenSource _cts = new();

    // Controls the Runs polling loop's lifetime independently of _cts — started
    // only while Home is visible, cancelled the moment it isn't, so no polling
    // happens in the background while a tab is focused.
    private FocusGatedPoller? _poller;

    // Home is visible only when no tab is active. Track the previous state so
    // we can trigger a data refresh when the user switches back to Home.
    private bool _wasVisible;

    // Each section shows a small spinner in its header (left of the label) until it
    // has loaded once, then swaps to the section icon — never re-blanks on later
    // visibility toggles. _runsLoading additionally flips the spinner back on during
    // every poll tick, since a Recent Runs refresh actually swaps the rendered rows.
    private bool _mapReady;
    private bool _runsReady;
    private bool _runsLoading;

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        var interval = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(RunsOpts.Value.PollingIntervalSeconds);
        _poller = new FocusGatedPoller(interval, async ct =>
        {
            await LoadRunsAsync();
            await InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged);
        }, ex => Logger.LogError(ex, "Runs polling failed"));

        EnvService.OnChange         += OnEnvExternalChange;
        HealthCache.OnHealthUpdated += OnHealthUpdated;
        TabService.OnChange         += OnTabVisibilityChanged;
        _wasVisible = TabService.ActiveTab is null;
        HealthCache.RequestRefresh(EnvService.SelectedId);
        await Task.WhenAll(LoadRunsAsync(), CheckHealthAsync());
        _runsReady = true;
        if (IsVisible) _poller.Start();
    }

    private void OnMapDataLoaded() => _ = InvokeAsync(() => { _mapReady = true; StateHasChanged(); });

    private bool IsVisible => TabService.ActiveTab is null;

    private void OnTabVisibilityChanged()
    {
        var visible = IsVisible;
        if (visible && !_wasVisible)
        {
            // Home just became visible — refresh stale data, render, then resume polling.
            _poller?.Start();
            _ = InvokeAsync(async () =>
            {
                try
                {
                    HealthCache.RequestRefresh(EnvService.SelectedId);
                    await Task.WhenAll(LoadRunsAsync(), CheckHealthAsync());
                    StateHasChanged();
                }
                catch (Exception ex) { Logger.LogError(ex, "Home visibility refresh failed"); }
            });
        }
        else if (!visible && _wasVisible)
        {
            // Home just became hidden — stop polling entirely, not just the render.
            _poller?.Stop();
        }
        _wasVisible = visible;
    }

    // ── Batch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private async Task LoadRunsAsync()
    {
        _runsLoading = true;
        try
        {
            _recent = await RunService.GetRunsAsync(
                EnvService.SelectedId, DateTime.UtcNow, HomeOpts.Value.RecentRunsCount, ct: _cts.Token);
        }
        catch (OperationCanceledException) { }
        catch (Exception ex) { Logger.LogError(ex, "LoadRunsAsync failed"); }
        finally { _runsLoading = false; }
    }

    // ── Health ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private async Task CheckHealthAsync()
    {
        var env = EnvService.SelectedId;
        _kafkaHealth = HealthCache.GetKafka(env);
        _mongoHealth = HealthCache.GetMongo(env);
        await InvokeAsync(() => { if (IsVisible) StateHasChanged(); });
    }

    private void OnHealthUpdated(string env)
    {
        if (env != EnvService.SelectedId) return;
        _ = InvokeAsync(async () =>
        {
            try { await CheckHealthAsync(); }
            catch (Exception ex) { Logger.LogError(ex, "Health update failed"); }
        });
    }

    // ── Env change ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private void OnEnvExternalChange()
    {
        _ = InvokeAsync(async () =>
        {
            try
            {
                _recent       = [];
                _runsReady    = false;
                _mapReady     = false; // new env's treemap has no cached paint yet
                if (IsVisible) StateHasChanged();
                HealthCache.RequestRefresh(EnvService.SelectedId);
                await Task.WhenAll(LoadRunsAsync(), CheckHealthAsync());
                _runsReady = true;
                if (IsVisible) StateHasChanged();
            }
            catch (Exception ex) { Logger.LogError(ex, "Env change update failed"); }
        });
    }

    private async Task OnEnvChanged(string newId)
    {
        EnvService.SelectedId = newId;
        _recent               = [];
        _runsReady            = false;
        _mapReady             = false;
        HealthCache.RequestRefresh(newId);
        await Task.WhenAll(LoadRunsAsync(), CheckHealthAsync());
        _runsReady = true;
    }

    // ── Navigation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private void OpenRunsDashboard() =>
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateRunsDashboard(EnvService.SelectedId));

    private void OpenServices() =>
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateServicesDashboard(EnvService.SelectedId));


    private void OnTreemapPidClicked(string key)
    {
        var tab = TabModel.CreateMemoryGraph(EnvService.SelectedId);
        tab.NavigationHint = key;
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(tab);
    }

    private void OpenDetail(RunSummary b) =>
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateRunDetail(b.RunId, b.RunId, EnvService.SelectedId));

    private void OpenTimeline(RunSummary b) =>
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateTimeline(b.RunId, EnvService.SelectedId));

    private async Task StopRunAsync(RunSummary b)
    {
        var (ok, message) = await RunActions.CancelRunAsync(EnvService.SelectedId, b.RunId, _cts.Token);
        if (ok)
        {
            Snackbar.Add(message, Severity.Warning);
            await LoadRunsAsync();
        }
        else
            Notifications.Report($"Could not stop run {b.RunId}: {message}", source: "Home");
    }

    // ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    private static string StatusCss(HealthStatus? s) => s switch
    {
        HealthStatus.Healthy            => "healthy",
        HealthStatus.Degraded           => "degraded",
        HealthStatus.DegradedEscalating => "degraded",
        HealthStatus.Down               => "down",
        _                                => "down", // null (not loaded yet) reads the same as Down/red
    };

    private static string DotCss(HealthStatus s) => s switch
    {
        HealthStatus.Healthy            => "bm-infra-dot-ok",
        HealthStatus.Degraded           => "bm-infra-dot-warn",
        HealthStatus.DegradedEscalating => "bm-infra-dot-warn bm-infra-dot-pulse",
        _                                => "bm-infra-dot-err",
    };

    // Hover reason shown on both the summary dot and the status label.
    private static string ReasonTip(HealthStatus? status, string? error) => status switch
    {
        HealthStatus.Healthy => "ping ok",
        null                  => "not checked yet",
        _                     => error ?? StatusLabel(status),
    };

    // Short display text for the status label — DegradedEscalating shares Degraded's
    // wording ("DEGRADED") since the blinking dot is what signals "worse than a one-off
    // blip"; spelling out the enum name (DEGRADEDESCALATING) would just look like a typo.
    private static string StatusLabel(HealthStatus? s) => s switch
    {
        HealthStatus.Healthy            => "HEALTHY",
        HealthStatus.Degraded           => "DEGRADED",
        HealthStatus.DegradedEscalating => "DEGRADED",
        HealthStatus.Down               => "DOWN",
        _                                 => "…",
    };

    public void Dispose()
    {
        EnvService.OnChange         -= OnEnvExternalChange;
        HealthCache.OnHealthUpdated -= OnHealthUpdated;
        TabService.OnChange         -= OnTabVisibilityChanged;
        _poller?.Stop();
        _cts.Cancel();
    }
}
FILE src/NxtUI.Web/Pages/HomeMemoryTreemap.razor
LENGTH 4506
@namespace NxtUI.Web.Pages
@using NxtUI.Core.Services
@using NxtUI.Web.Services
@inject IServiceMetricsMonitor MetricsMonitor
@inject TabService TabService
@inject IJSRuntime JS
@implements IAsyncDisposable

<div class="bm-home-treemap-wrap">
    <!-- The loading spinner + message are injected by d3-home-treemap.js itself
         (into _ref, cleared on first paint) — do not duplicate it here. -->
    <div class="bm-home-treemap" @ref="_ref"></div>
</div>

@code {
    [Parameter] public string            Env            { get; set; } = string.Empty;

    // Fires on the first successful paint (something is on screen) — use this to
    // gate a "map is ready" loading spinner. OnDataLoaded fires later, only once
    // every online leaf has reported a RAM sample — real infra may have services
    // that never report RAM, so a spinner gated on OnDataLoaded can hang forever.
    [Parameter] public EventCallback     OnFirstPaint   { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback     OnDataLoaded   { get; set; }
    [Parameter] public EventCallback<string> OnPidClicked  { get; set; }

    // When false (e.g. Home is hidden behind another tab), the JS polling loop is
    // stopped entirely — no background fetches while nobody can see the result.
    [Parameter] public bool Visible { get; set; } = true;

    private ElementReference                        _ref;
    private IDisposable?                            _sub;
    private DotNetObjectReference<HomeMemoryTreemap>? _selfRef;
    private string                                  _subscribedEnv = string.Empty;
    private bool                                    _started;
    private bool                                    _wasVisible = true;

    protected override async Task OnParametersSetAsync()
    {
        if (Env != _subscribedEnv)
        {
            _sub?.Dispose();
            _sub = MetricsMonitor.Subscribe(Env);
            _subscribedEnv = Env;

            if (_started)
            {
                // Env changed — clear old tree and start new. start() re-injects its
                // own loading spinner into the container immediately (no cached data
                // exists yet for a never-seen env).
                try { await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("homeMemoryTreemap.stop", _ref); } catch (Exception) { }
                _selfRef ??= DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);
                await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("homeMemoryTreemap.start", _ref, Env, null, _selfRef, _selfRef);
            }
        }

        if (Visible != _wasVisible)
        {
            if (!Visible && _started)
            {
                // Home just became hidden — stop polling entirely.
                try { await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("homeMemoryTreemap.stop", _ref); } catch (Exception) { }
            }
            else if (Visible && _started)
            {
                // Home just became visible again — resume. start() repaints from
                // cached data instantly (no spinner) if we've fetched this env before.
                _selfRef ??= DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);
                await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("homeMemoryTreemap.start", _ref, Env, null, _selfRef, _selfRef);
            }
            _wasVisible = Visible;
        }
    }

    protected override async Task OnAfterRenderAsync(bool firstRender)
    {
        if (!firstRender || _started) return;
        _started = true;
        _selfRef ??= DotNetObjectReference.Create(this);
        await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("homeMemoryTreemap.start", _ref, Env, null, _selfRef, _selfRef);
        await InvokeAsync(StateHasChanged);
    }

    [JSInvokable]
    public void NotifyFirstPaint() => _ = OnFirstPaint.InvokeAsync();

    [JSInvokable]
    public void NotifyDataLoaded() => _ = OnDataLoaded.InvokeAsync();

    [JSInvokable]
    public void OnLeafClicked(string key) => _ = OnPidClicked.InvokeAsync(key);

    private void OpenServices() =>
        TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateServicesDashboard(Env));

    public ValueTask DisposeAsync()
    {
        // Synchronous — releases the subscription and .NET object reference, no I/O.
        _sub?.Dispose();
        _selfRef?.Dispose();

        // Fire-and-forget: client-side D3 cleanup for a container Blazor removes anyway.
        _ = SafeStopAsync();
        return ValueTask.CompletedTask;
    }

    private async Task SafeStopAsync()
    {
        try { await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("homeMemoryTreemap.stop", _ref); } catch (Exception) { }
    }
}

@namespace NxtUI.Web.Pages
@using NxtUI.Core.Models

<div class="bm-page bm-page-fill @(_viewMode == KafkaView.Consumers ? "bm-inspector" : "")">

    @if (_viewMode == KafkaView.Consumers)

                <HeaderContent>

@code {
    private enum KafkaView { Topics, Consumers }

    [Parameter] public string Environment { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    [Parameter] public TabModel? Tab { get; set; }

    // Shared
    private KafkaClusterInfo? _cluster  = null;
    private KafkaView         _viewMode = KafkaView.Topics;
    private bool              _loading  = true;
    private bool              _enriching = false;
    private string            _filterText       = string.Empty;
    private string?           _filterParseError = null;
    private CancellationTokenSource _cts = new();

    // Topics
    private List<KafkaTopicSummary> _allTopics      = new();
    private List<KafkaTopicSummary> _filteredTopics = new();
    private List<KafkaTopicSummary> _paged          = new();
    private int _page = 1, _pageSize = 25, _totalPages = 1;

    // Client-side sort state for the topic table. Because the whole topic set lives in
    // memory (_filteredTopics), the sort must be applied to that full set before it's
    // sliced into the current page — otherwise it would only reorder the visible page.
    private string _sortColumn     = "Name";
    private bool   _sortDescending = false;

    // Consumers
    private List<KafkaConsumerGroupOverview> _allGroups      = new();
    private List<KafkaConsumerGroupOverview> _filteredGroups = new();
    private KafkaConsumerGroupOverview?      _selectedGroup  = null;
    private List<KafkaGroupTopicLag>?        _topicLags      = null;
    private bool _loadingDetail = false;

    // Filter parsers
    private static readonly FilterParser TopicParser = new(["Name", "PartitionCount", "MessageCount"],
        new Dictionary<string, string>
        {
            ["name"]       = "Name",
            ["topic"]      = "Name",
            ["partitions"] = "PartitionCount",
            ["parts"]      = "PartitionCount",
            ["messages"]   = "MessageCount",
            ["msgs"]       = "MessageCount",
        });

    private static readonly FilterParser GroupParser = new(["GroupId", "State"],
        new Dictionary<string, string>
        {
            ["group"]   = "GroupId",
            ["groupid"] = "GroupId",
            ["state"]   = "State",
        });

    private static readonly (string Aliases, string Label)[] _topicFieldHints =
    [
        ("name / topic",         "topic name"),
        ("partitions / parts",   "partition count (supports >, >=, <, <=, ..)"),
        ("messages / msgs",      "message count (supports >, >=, <, <=, ..)"),
    ];

    private static readonly (string Aliases, string Label)[] _groupFieldHints =
    [
        ("group / groupid", "consumer group ID"),
        ("state",           "Stable / Rebalancing / Dead / Empty"),
    ];

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync() => await LoadAsync();

    private async Task LoadAsync()
    {
        _loading   = true;
        _enriching = false;
        using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
        StateHasChanged();
        try
        {
            var ct = _cts.Token;

            if (_viewMode == KafkaView.Topics)
            {
                // Phase 1 — cluster info and topic list in parallel; render cluster bar as
                // soon as GetClusterInfoAsync resolves (don't block on topics).
                var clusterTask = KafkaSvc.GetClusterInfoAsync(Environment, ct);
                var topicsTask  = KafkaSvc.GetTopicsAsync(Environment, ct);

                _cluster = await clusterTask;
                StateHasChanged(); // show cluster bar immediately

                _allTopics = (await topicsTask).ToList();
                ApplyFilter();
                _loading   = false;
                _enriching = true;
                StateHasChanged(); // show topic table (partitions already known) while enrichment loads

                // Phase 2 — cleanup policy (for the "~" compacted marker) + an estimated
                // message count, for every topic in one batched call (a handful of broker
                // round trips total, not one AdminClient + one-or-two round trips per topic).
                var enrichment = await KafkaSvc.GetTopicEnrichmentAsync(Environment, _allTopics, ct);
                if (!ct.IsCancellationRequested)
                {
                    for (var i = 0; i < _allTopics.Count; i++)
                    {
                        if (!enrichment.TryGetValue(_allTopics[i].Name, out var e)) continue;
                        _allTopics[i] = _allTopics[i] with
                        {
                            CleanupPolicy = e.CleanupPolicy,
                            MessageCount  = e.MessageCount,
                            ConfigLoaded  = true,
                        };
                    }
                    ApplyFilter();
                }
            }
            else
            {
                // Consumers: cluster info and group list in parallel; render cluster bar first.
                var clusterTask = KafkaSvc.GetClusterInfoAsync(Environment, ct);
                var groupsTask  = KafkaSvc.GetAllConsumerGroupsAsync(Environment, ct);

                _cluster = await clusterTask;
                StateHasChanged();

                _allGroups     = (await groupsTask).ToList();
                _selectedGroup = null;
                _topicLags     = null;
                ApplyFilter();
            }
        }
        catch (OperationCanceledException) { }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Logger.LogError(ex, "Failed to load Kafka {ViewMode} for env {Env}", _viewMode, Environment);
            Notifications.Report($"Failed to load Kafka data: {ex.Message}", ex, "Kafka Dashboard");
        }
        finally
        {
            _loading   = false;
            _enriching = false;
            StateHasChanged();
        }
    }

    private void SwitchView(KafkaView view)
    {
        if (_viewMode == view) return;
        _viewMode         = view;
        _filterText       = string.Empty;
        _filterParseError = null;
        _selectedGroup    = null;
        _topicLags        = null;
        _ = LoadAsync();
    }

    private void OnFilterChanged(string text)
    {
        _filterText       = text;
        _filterParseError = null;
        FilterNode? node  = null;
        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text))
        {
            try
            {
                node = (_viewMode == KafkaView.Topics ? TopicParser : GroupParser).Parse(text, DtSvc.UseUtc);
            }
            catch (FilterParseException ex) { _filterParseError = ex.Message; }
        }

        if (_viewMode == KafkaView.Topics)
        {
            _filteredTopics = node is null
                ? _allTopics
                : _allTopics.Where(t => FilterEvaluator.Evaluate(node, t)).ToList();
            _page = 1;
            RebuildPage();
        }
        else
        {
            _filteredGroups = node is null
                ? _allGroups
                : _allGroups.Where(g => FilterEvaluator.Evaluate(node, g)).ToList();
        }
    }

    private void ApplyFilter() => OnFilterChanged(_filterText);

    private void RebuildPage()
    {
        // Sort the full filtered set first, THEN slice the page out of it.
        var sorted = SortTopics(_filteredTopics);
        _totalPages = Math.Max(1, (int)Math.Ceiling(sorted.Count / (double)_pageSize));
        _page       = Math.Clamp(_page, 1, _totalPages);
        _paged      = sorted.Skip((_page - 1) * _pageSize).Take(_pageSize).ToList();
    }

    private List<KafkaTopicSummary> SortTopics(List<KafkaTopicSummary> topics)
    {
        var sorted = _sortColumn switch
        {
            "PartitionCount" => _sortDescending
                ? topics.OrderByDescending(t => t.PartitionCount)
                : topics.OrderBy(t => t.PartitionCount),
            "MessageCount" => _sortDescending
                ? topics.OrderByDescending(t => t.MessageCount)
                : topics.OrderBy(t => t.MessageCount),
            _ => _sortDescending
                ? topics.OrderByDescending(t => t.Name, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
                : topics.OrderBy(t => t.Name, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase),
        };
        return sorted.ToList();
    }

    private void OnTopicSort(string field)
    {
        if (_sortColumn == field) _sortDescending = !_sortDescending;
        else { _sortColumn = field; _sortDescending = false; }
        RebuildPage();
    }

    private RenderFragment SortHeader(string label, string field) => @<MudLink
        Underline="Underline.Hover" Color="Color.Inherit"
        OnClick="@(() => OnTopicSort(field))"
        Style="cursor:pointer; display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:2px;">
        @label
        @if (_sortColumn == field)
        {
            <MudIcon Icon="@(_sortDescending ? Icons.Material.Filled.ArrowDownward : Icons.Material.Filled.ArrowUpward)"
                     Size="Size.Small" />
        }
    </MudLink>;

    private void OnPageChanged(int page)   { _page = page;  RebuildPage(); }
    private void OnPageSizeChanged(int sz) { _pageSize = sz; _page = 1; RebuildPage(); }

    private async Task OnGroupSelected(KafkaConsumerGroupOverview? group)
    {
        if (group is null || ReferenceEquals(_selectedGroup, group))
        {
            _selectedGroup = null;
            _topicLags     = null;
            StateHasChanged();
            return;
        }
        _selectedGroup = group;
        _topicLags     = null;
        _loadingDetail = true;
        using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
        StateHasChanged();
        try
        {
            var lags   = await KafkaSvc.GetGroupTopicLagsAsync(Environment, group.GroupId, _cts.Token);
            _topicLags = lags.ToList();
        }
        catch (OperationCanceledException) { }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Logger.LogError(ex, "Failed to load lags for group {GroupId}", group.GroupId);
            Notifications.Report($"Failed to load lag data for \"{group.GroupId}\": {ex.Message}", ex, "Kafka Dashboard");
        }
        finally
        {
            _loadingDetail = false;
            StateHasChanged();
        }
    }

    private async Task<bool> ConfirmAsync(string message, string confirmText = "Delete")
    {
        var dialog = await DialogService.ShowAsync<ConfirmDialog>("Confirm", new DialogParameters<ConfirmDialog>
        {
            { x => x.Message,     message     },
            { x => x.ConfirmText, confirmText },
        }, new DialogOptions { MaxWidth = MaxWidth.ExtraSmall });
        var result = await dialog.Result;
        return result is { Canceled: false };
    }

    private async Task OpenPurgeTopicsDialogAsync()
    {
        var parameters = new DialogParameters<BulkActionDialog>
        {
            { x => x.ActionVerb,        "Purge" },
            { x => x.ButtonColor,       Color.Error },
            { x => x.ItemLabel,         "Kafka topic" },
            { x => x.LoadItemsAsync,    () => Task.FromResult(_allTopics.Select(t => t.Name).ToList()) },
            { x => x.Endpoint,          "api/kafka/purge" },
            { x => x.BuildRequestBody,  (List<string> items) => new { Env = Environment, Items = items } },
        };
        await DialogService.ShowAsync<BulkActionDialog>("Purge Kafka topics", parameters,
            new DialogOptions { MaxWidth = MaxWidth.Small, FullWidth = true, CloseButton = true });
    }

    private async Task DeleteTopicAsync(string topicName)
    {
        if (!await ConfirmAsync($"Delete topic \"{topicName}\"? This permanently removes all messages and cannot be undone.")) return;
        try
        {
            await KafkaSvc.DeleteTopicAsync(Environment, topicName, _cts.Token);
            Snackbar.Add($"Topic \"{topicName}\" deleted.", Severity.Success);
        }
        catch
        {
            try
            {
                await KafkaSvc.SetTopicRetentionAsync(Environment, topicName, 1, _cts.Token);
                Snackbar.Add($"Could not delete \"{topicName}\" — retention set to 1 ms (messages will expire shortly).", Severity.Warning);
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Notifications.Report($"Failed to delete or clear topic \"{topicName}\".", ex, "Kafka Dashboard");
                return;
            }
        }
        await LoadAsync();
    }

    private async Task DeleteConsumerGroupAsync(string groupId)
    {
        if (!await ConfirmAsync($"Delete consumer group \"{groupId}\"? Offsets for this group will be lost.")) return;
        try
        {
            await KafkaSvc.DeleteConsumerGroupAsync(Environment, groupId, _cts.Token);
            if (_selectedGroup?.GroupId == groupId) { _selectedGroup = null; _topicLags = null; }
            Snackbar.Add($"Consumer group \"{groupId}\" deleted.", Severity.Success);
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            Notifications.Report($"Failed to delete consumer group \"{groupId}\".", ex, "Kafka Dashboard");
            return;
        }
        await LoadAsync();
    }

    private async Task OpenInfoDialog(KafkaTopicSummary topic)
    {
        var p = new DialogParameters<KafkaTopicInfoDialog>
        {
            { x => x.TopicName,    topic.Name  },
            { x => x.Environment,  Environment },
            { x => x.MessageCount, topic.ConfigLoaded ? FormatMessages(topic) : null },
        };
        await DialogService.ShowAsync<KafkaTopicInfoDialog>($"{topic.Name} — config", p,
            new DialogOptions { MaxWidth = MaxWidth.Small, FullWidth = true, CloseButton = true });
    }

    private async Task OpenGroupsDialog(string topicName)
    {
        var p = new DialogParameters<KafkaConsumerGroupsDialog>
        {
            { x => x.TopicName,   topicName   },
            { x => x.Environment, Environment },
        };
        await DialogService.ShowAsync<KafkaConsumerGroupsDialog>($"{topicName} — consumer groups", p,
            new DialogOptions { MaxWidth = MaxWidth.Small, FullWidth = true, CloseButton = true });
    }

    private void OpenInspector(string topicName)
        => TabService.OpenOrFocus(TabModel.CreateKafkaTopicInspector(topicName, Environment));

    private static string FormatMessages(KafkaTopicSummary t)
    {
        var prefix = t.CleanupPolicy.Contains("compact") ? "~" : "";
        return prefix + t.MessageCount.ToString("N0");
    }

    private static Color StateColor(string state) => state switch
    {
        "Stable"      => Color.Success,
        "Rebalancing" => Color.Warning,
        "Dead"        => Color.Error,
        _             => Color.Default,
    };

    public void Dispose() => _cts.Cancel();
}

<div class="bm-page bm-page-fill bm-inspector">

@code {
    [Parameter] public string Environment { get; set; } = string.Empty;
    [Parameter] public TabModel? Tab { get; set; }

    private List<KafkaConsumerGroupOverview>  _all      = new();
    private List<KafkaConsumerGroupOverview>  _filtered = new();
    private KafkaConsumerGroupOverview?              _selected = null;
    private Result<List<KafkaConsumerGroupOverview>> _result;
    private Result<List<KafkaGroupTopicLag>>         _detailResult;
    private string  _filterText       = string.Empty;
    private string? _filterParseError = null;
    private CancellationTokenSource _cts = new();

    private static readonly string[] SearchableFields = ["GroupId", "State"];
    private static readonly FilterParser Parser = new(SearchableFields,
        new Dictionary<string, string>
        {
            ["group"]   = "GroupId",
            ["groupid"] = "GroupId",
            ["state"]   = "State",
        });

    private static readonly (string Aliases, string Label)[] _fieldHints =
    [
        ("group / groupid", "consumer group ID"),
        ("state",           "Stable / Rebalancing / Dead / Empty"),
    ];

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync() => await LoadAsync();

    private async Task LoadAsync()
    {
        _result       = default; // loading
        _selected     = null;
        _detailResult = default;
        using var loadingScope = TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService);
        StateHasChanged();
        try
        {
            var groups = await KafkaSvc.GetAllConsumerGroupsAsync(Environment, _cts.Token);
            _all = groups.ToList();
            ApplyFilter();
            _result = Result<List<KafkaConsumerGroupOverview>>.Ok(_all);
        }
        catch (OperationCanceledException) { }
        catch (Exception ex) { _result = Result<List<KafkaConsumerGroupOverview>>.Fail(ex.Message); }
    }

    private void OnFilterChanged(string text)
    {
        _filterText = text;
        _filterParseError = null;
        FilterNode? node = null;
        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(text))
        {
            try   { node = Parser.Parse(text, DtSvc.UseUtc); }
            catch (FilterParseException ex) { _filterParseError = ex.Message; }
        }
        _filtered = node is null
            ? _all
            : _all.Where(g => FilterEvaluator.Evaluate(node, g)).ToList();
    }

    private void ApplyFilter() => OnFilterChanged(_filterText);

    private async Task OnRowSelected(KafkaConsumerGroupOverview? group)
    {
        if (group is null || ReferenceEquals(_selected, group))
        {
            _selected     = null;
            _detailResult = default;
            StateHasChanged();
            return;
        }
        _selected     = group;
        _detailResult = default; // loading
        using (TabLoadingScope.Begin(Tab, TabService))
        {
            StateHasChanged();
            try
            {
                var lags  = await KafkaSvc.GetGroupTopicLagsAsync(Environment, group.GroupId, _cts.Token);
                _detailResult = Result<List<KafkaGroupTopicLag>>.Ok(lags.ToList());
            }
            catch (OperationCanceledException) { }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Logger.LogError(ex, "Failed to load lags for group {GroupId}", group.GroupId);
                _detailResult = Result<List<KafkaGroupTopicLag>>.Fail(ex.Message);
            }
        }
        StateHasChanged();
    }

    private static Color StateColor(string state) => state switch
    {
        "Stable"      => Color.Success,
        "Rebalancing" => Color.Warning,
        "Dead"        => Color.Error,
        "Empty"       => Color.Default,
        _             => Color.Default,
    };

    public void Dispose() => _cts.Cancel();
}
